title: Tyson pork plant in Indiana suspended from exporting to China -USDA
text: Tyson Foods Inc's pork processing plant in Logansport, Indiana, has been suspended from exporting products to China, the U.S. Department of Agriculture said on Monday.

title: Tyson Foods (TSN) Benefits From Capacity Expansion, High Demand
text: Tyson Foods (TSN) is undertaking several operational and supply chain efficiency programs to place itself better in the long run. Also, its retail business is a key driver.

title: Tyson Foods: Market Overreaction After Q3
text: Tyson Foods sales are up 8% from Q3 2021 and up 16% from YTD 2021. Beef and pork are responsible for about 50% of Tyson Foods' revenues, and demand for both segments is on a long-term downward trend, especially beef.

title: Tyson: Does The 10% Drop Following Q3 Earnings Signal A Buy Or A Sell?
text: Tyson's recent results succumbed to macroeconomic headwinds. Some segments suffered large double-digit declines in operating income.

title: Tyson Foods Demonstrates Commitment to Credible Journalism with Mindshare and SocialContext Impact Index
text: Impact Index to Drive Socially Responsible Brand Content and Media Investments SPRINGDALE, Ark. , Aug. 11, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Tyson Foods, Inc. (NYSE: TSN) is demonstrating its commitment to credible and socially responsible journalism in its advertising on websites and media by conducting a social context Impact Index in partnership with Mindshare, the global media services company, and socialcontext.ai, a contextual intelligence startup supported by the University of Colorado Boulder.

title: Wonder Bread Maker's Earnings Due, As Food Prices Take A Bite Out Of Tyson

title: Tyson raises prices of chicken as demand shifts from expensive beef cuts
text: Tyson Foods reported that it had strong demand in its chicken category as well as cheaper cuts of meat, such as breakfast sausage and bacon, as grocery prices continue to rise.

title: Tyson Foods (TSN) Q3 Earnings Top Estimates, Sales Up Y/Y
text: Tyson Foods' (TSN) third-quarter fiscal 2022 sales increase year over year on the back of gains across most segments. However, earnings decline in the quarter.

title: Is Tyson Foods A Buy On Post-Earnings Weakness?
text: Shares of Tyson are down hard in the wake of the Q3 results but may be offering an entry point to value-minded income investors.

title: Tyson Foods (TSN) Q3 Earnings and Revenues Surpass Estimates
text: Tyson (TSN) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of 1.57% and 1.41%, respectively, for the quarter ended June 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: Tyson Foods profit misses but revenue rises above expectations, as higher pricing offset volume decline
text: Shares of Tyson Foods Inc. TSN, +1.02% fell 0.9% in premarket trading Monday, after the meats and processed foods company reported fiscal third-quarter profit that missed expectations while revenue beat, as higher pricing helped offset volume declines. Net income for the quarter to July 2 rose to $750 million, or $2.07 a share, from $749 million, or $2.05 a share, in the year-ago period.

title: Tyson Foods beats quarterly revenue estimates
text: Tyson Foods Inc quarterly revenue beat estimates on Monday, as demand for its higher-priced chicken and beef remained steady in the face of decades-high inflation.

title: Tyson Foods (TSN) Queued for Q3 Earnings: Things to Consider

title: Earnings Previews: Barrick Gold, Palantir, Tyson Foods, Viatris
text: The three major U.S. equity indexes closed higher Wednesday. The Dow Jones industrials added 1.29%, the S&P 500 increased by 1.56%, and the Nasdaq jumped by 2.59%.

title: Tyson Foods ignoring subpoena for meat price gouging probe, NY attorney general says
text: Tyson Foods Inc, one of the largest U.S. meat producers, is refusing to comply with a subpoena for a civil probe into possible price gouging during the COVID-19 pandemic, New York's attorney general said on Wednesday.
